summary: Sima Yi; the High Plains Tomb coup (Gaoping Ling). With Cao Shuang and the new emperor Cao Fang away at the imperial cemetery, Sima Yi (long feigning illness — receives Li Sheng with the famous bedridden-doddering act of dribbling porridge down his front to deceive the spies) suddenly seizes the capital. Closes the gates; secures Empress Guo's authority; charges Cao Shuang with treason. Cao Shuang surrenders on Sima Yi's promise of mercy; promptly executed with three clans wiped out. Sima Yi takes Wei. The coup that founds Jin.

Cao Shuang; the chapter's victim. The dominant regent who has crowded out Sima Yi for a decade; surrounded by the 'Five Hubs' faction (He Yan, Deng Yang, Ding Mi, Li Sheng, Bi Gui). Tricked by Sima Yi's feigned senility; goes to the cemetery; loses Luoyang behind him; refuses Huan Fan's counsel to take the emperor to Xuchang and resist; surrenders on Sima Yi's word of mercy; betrayed and executed with all his kin.
Huan Fan; urges Cao Shuang to flee with Cao Fang to Xuchang and fight; ignored; executed with the Cao Shuang clan.
He Yan; one of the Five Hubs; executed with Cao Shuang.
Deng Yang; one of the Five Hubs; executed.
Ding Mi; one of the Five Hubs; executed.
Li Sheng; one of the Five Hubs; sent earlier as a spy to Sima Yi's sickbed; fooled by the porridge-and-drool act; executed.
Bi Gui; one of the Five Hubs; executed.
Zhang Dang; the eunuch turncoat; testifies against Cao Shuang.
Cao Fang; the boy emperor; reigns nominally; powerless puppet through the rest of his time on the throne. Will be deposed by Sima Shi in chapter 109.
Cao Rui; named in passing — already dead; the recap of the regency arrangement.
Empress Guo; widow of Cao Rui; the dowager whose authority Sima Yi takes for the coup.
Sima Shi; with his father in the coup; takes command of the palace troops.
Sima Zhao; with his father in the coup.
Guan Lu; the diviner; in the chapter's first half predicts Cao Shuang's fall. The portent foreshadowing the coup.
Cao Xun; Cao Shuang's brother; executed.
Cao Yan; Cao Shuang's brother; executed.
Chen Qun; named in passing — the old regent.
Xiahou Ba; Cao Shuang's brother-in-arms; defects to Shu after the coup. Becomes the chapter's bridge to Shu's western front; brings intelligence to Jiang Wei.
Xiahou Wei; with the Wei court.
Empress Mao; named in passing — Cao Rui's deposed empress.
Cao Zhen; named in passing — long dead.
Meng Da; named in passing — long dead.
Liu Fang; in the Wei court.
Liu Fei; in the Wei court.
Sun Quan; in Wu; receives news of the Wei coup.
Bei Yan; minor figure named in passing.
Gongsun Yuan; named in passing in the recap of his Liaodong revolt.
Gongsun Du; Gongsun Yuan's grandfather; named in passing in the Liaodong recap.
Hu Zun; with Sima Yi in the Liaodong campaign recap.
Xu Yan; with the Wei force.
Yuan Shang; named in passing in the Liaodong recap.
Yuan Xi; named in passing in the Liaodong recap.
Zhang Hu; with the Wei force.

Sima Yi; consolidates the coup; appoints his own men through the Wei government; dies of old age toward the chapter's end. Hands command to Sima Shi. The figure who has stalked Zhuge Liang through every late chapter passes to his sons.
Sima Shi; succeeds his father as Grand Marshal of Wei. Will depose Cao Fang and govern Wei in his own right.
Sima Zhao; with his brother; appointed Grand General.

Jiang Wei; mounts a Northern Expedition with the briefing from Xiahou Ba on Wei's internal divisions; attacks at the western frontier. Defeated by Guo Huai and forced to withdraw — the first of Jiang Wei's many campaigns.
Xiahou Ba; the defector to Shu; briefs Jiang Wei on the Wei court situation; serves alongside Jiang Wei through the next several campaigns.
Guo Huai; defeats Jiang Wei's western expedition.

Deng Ai; first major appearance — Sima Yi's protégé. Brilliant strategist on the Wei western front; will be the dominant Wei figure against Jiang Wei through the next decade and the eventual conqueror of Shu.
Chen Tai; rising Wei officer; serves under Guo Huai.
Cao Fang; the puppet emperor; powerless under Sima Shi.
Cao Shuang; named in passing — the executed regent.
Jiang Wan; in Liu Shan's court.
Fei Yi; in Liu Shan's court.
Dong Yun; in Liu Shan's court.
Sima Fu; in the Wei court.
Jiang Ji; in the Wei court.
Gao Rou; in the Wei court.
Zhong Hui; first major appearance — Zhong Yao's son; the brilliant young Wei official rising. Will be one of the two co-commanders of the eventual Shu conquest.
Zhong Yao; named in passing — Zhong Hui's father; already dead.
Huan Fan; named in passing — executed.
He Yan; named in passing — executed.
Deng Yang; named in passing — executed.
Ding Mi; named in passing — executed.
Li Sheng; named in passing — executed.
Bi Gui; named in passing — executed.
Cao Xun; named in passing — executed.
Cao Yan; named in passing — executed.
Xin Xianying; Xin Chang's wise sister; advises him to commit to neither side.
Lady Xiahou; widow of one of the executed.
Zhuge Liang; named in passing in the recap.
Sun Quan dies. Aged 71. The last of the founding Three Kingdoms monarchs gone. Sun Liang succeeds as the new emperor of Wu; Zhuge Ke is named regent. The era closes.
Sun Liang; the boy emperor of Wu; succeeds Sun Quan.

Zhuge Ke; Zhuge Jin's brilliant son; appointed regent of Wu by Sun Quan's deathbed. Immediately defeats Wei in his first campaign — Sima Zhao's invasion of Dongxing Pass — by Ding Feng's famous winter charge through the snow stripped to undershirts.

Ding Feng; the chapter's hero. At Dongxing Pass, ordered by Zhuge Ke to lead the assault, has his men strip to undershirts in the dead of winter and charge bare-armed across the snow into the Wei camps. The Wei pickets, drunk and underdressed, are run over. The novel's iconic 'cold-steel-in-the-snow' charge.
Sima Zhao; invades Wu through Dongxing Pass; defeated by Ding Feng's charge; retreats.
Sima Shi; in the Wei capital.
Sima Yi; named in passing — already dead.
Guo Huai; on the Wei front.
Hu Zun; Wei commander; defeated at Dongxing.

Zhang Te; defends Xincheng against Zhuge Ke's siege at chapter's end with a desperate last-stand defense — feigns surrender to buy three days; Zhuge Ke's force is decimated by plague during the delay.
Sun Deng; named in passing — Sun Quan's first heir, already dead.
Sun He; named in passing — the deposed crown prince.
Sun Jian; named in passing — the founding ancestor.
Sun Jun; the Sun-clan officer who will assassinate Zhuge Ke in chapter 109. Watch this one.
Lu Xun; named in passing — long dead by this point.
Zhuge Jin; named in passing — Zhuge Ke's father, already dead.
Tang Zi; with the Wei force.
Teng Yin; in the Wu court.
Lady Xu; in the Wu court.

Jiang Wei; mounts another Northern Expedition, the second. Engages Sima Zhao at Tiehlung Mountain — the famous boxed-in encounter where Sima Zhao is trapped on the height by Jiang Wei but escapes via the well-water prayer that produces a miraculous spring.

Sima Zhao; trapped on Tiehlung Mountain by Jiang Wei with no water; prays at the mountain shrine; a spring of water appears at the foot of the rock; saves himself and his men. The divine-water episode.

Sima Shi; the chapter's centerpiece. Deposes Cao Fang for an alleged plot with Li Feng, Xiahou Xuan, and Empress Zhang's father Zhang Ji (Wei dynasty); executes them all; installs Cao Mao as the new emperor.
Cao Fang; deposed by Sima Shi; sent home with the rank of Prince of Qi. The boy emperor's reign ends.
Cao Mao; the new emperor, installed by Sima Shi. Will be murdered by Sima Zhao's men in chapter 114 trying to break the Sima yoke.
Li Feng; allegedly plots with Cao Fang against Sima Shi; executed with his family.
Xiahou Xuan; allegedly plots with Li Feng; executed. The last of the once-powerful Xiahou clan brought down.
Empress Zhang; Cao Fang's empress; deposed when her father is exposed in the plot.
Xiahou Ba; on the Shu side; serves Jiang Wei.
Liao Hua; with Jiang Wei's expedition.
Guo Huai dies. The veteran Wei western commander; killed in this chapter — shot by an arrow at the western front engagement.
Chen Tai; Wei officer; rising as the front commander.
Sima Fu; in the Wei court.
Wang Su; in the Wei court.
Sima Yi; named in passing — already dead.
Guanqiu Jian; Wei commander; will rebel against Sima Shi in chapter 110.
Wen Qin; Wei commander; will join Guanqiu Jian's rebellion.
Dong Zhuo; named in passing in Cao Mao's accession history.
Dong Cheng; named in passing in the historical recap.
Xi Zheng; in Liu Shan's court.
Zhang Ni; on the Shu side.

Guanqiu Jian; rebels against Sima Shi at Shouchun. The chapter's first half. With Wen Qin and Wen Yang, raises the eastern garrison against the Sima regime. Defeated by Sima Shi (who comes personally despite a recent eye operation); Guanqiu Jian killed in flight.
Wen Qin; co-rebels with Guanqiu Jian; flees to Wu when the rebellion fails. Will return as a Wu commander later.

Wen Yang; the chapter's iconic teenage warrior. Wen Qin's 18-year-old son; at Le-jia (Yueja), in the dead of night, rides alone with a spear into the Wei army of tens of thousands camped before the rebel position; rides clean through, kills uncounted men, throws the camp into riot; escapes; the next morning is pursued by Wei cavalry — turns back and breaks the pursuit force seven times. The boy general who matches Zhao Yun at Changban; the late novel's last great single-rider feat.

Sima Shi; suppresses the Guanqiu Jian rebellion in person. The exertion bursts his eye-tumor (he had been recovering from eye surgery); dies of the wound on the campaign. End of the elder Sima brother's command; Sima Zhao inherits.
Sima Zhao; succeeds his brother as supreme commander of Wei; the dominant figure of the rest of the novel.
Jiang Wei; takes advantage of the Wei rebellion to launch yet another Northern Expedition. Defeats Wang Jing at the Tao River.
Wang Jing; Wei officer; defeated by Jiang Wei at the Tao River; the chapter's second half.
Chen Tai; senior Wei officer; relieves Wang Jing.
Deng Ai; arrives on the Wei front to counter Jiang Wei.
Cao Fang; named in passing — the deposed.
Guan Yu; named in passing in the eulogy for Wen Yang's exploit ('rivals the Lord of Changban').
Zhao Yun; named in passing in the same eulogy comparison.
Xiahou Ba; with Jiang Wei's expedition.
Zhuge Dan; named in passing as the next rebel candidate; will rise in chapter 111.
Guanqiu Dian; Guanqiu Jian's son; executed.
Wang Su; in the Wei court.
Xiahou Xuan; named in passing — executed last chapter.
Sun Jun; in Wu — the assassin of Zhuge Ke; named in passing here.
Sun Quan; named in passing — dead.
Li Feng; named in passing — executed.
Zhang Ni; with the Shu army.
Zhuge Liang; named in passing in the recap.
